summary: Novel multi-agent framework that dynamically routes tasks to appropriately-sized models based on task complexity and agent confidence signals, achieving 12.88% accuracy improvement and 79.78% cost reduction versus static model assignment.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses AgentForge's hardcoded AGENT_STAGES runtime mapping; AiRun already has plannerRuntime/executorRuntime fields ready for dynamic population

source: Circuit Breaker + Graceful Degradation for Agent Subprocesses
summary: Production patterns for multi-agent systems: exponential backoff with jitter and circuit breakers (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN states) eliminate 80-90% of transient AIBlocked events caused by environment noise in subprocess-based agent runtimes.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — ProcessRunner currently sends runs to AIBlocked on first subprocess crash with no retry; rehydrateOrphans() provides the detection foundation but no recovery

source: Karpathy AutoResearch — Ratchet Loop for Autonomous Agent Improvement
summary: Andrej Karpathy's AutoResearch (released March 7 2026, 66k+ stars) demonstrates a minimal ratchet loop: agent reads prior attempt results, proposes targeted repair, only keeps improvements. 700 experiments in 48 hours discovered 20 net optimizations.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — AgentForge has no plan repair; failed Implementing stage sends run to AIBlocked and discards artifact context. Ratchet loop maps directly to feeding ExecutionReport back to planReviser.

summary: Microsoft Research paper (March 2026) that hides tool latency by speculatively pre-executing likely future tool calls during LLM thinking time, reducing average task completion time by 48.5% and improving tool throughput by 1.8x.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — PASTE operates at the tool-call level inside an LLM agent, but AgentForge agents are black-box CLI subprocesses (stdin/stdout). Cannot intercept individual tool calls from the orchestrator level.

source: NVIDIA ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training
summary: NVIDIA's decoupled rollout-as-a-service infrastructure for RL training of multi-turn LLM agents. Asynchronous INIT/RUN/EVAL pipeline nearly doubled Qwen3-8B performance on SWE-Bench Verified from 9.6% to 18.0%.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — ProRL is infrastructure for training new agent models via RL, not for orchestrating existing CLI agents. AgentForge uses pre-trained Claude Code and Codex CLIs.

source: MIRIX: Multi-Agent Memory System for LLM-Based Agents
summary: Six-type memory architecture (Core, Episodic, Semantic, Procedural, Resource, Knowledge Vault) with a dedicated multi-agent memory management layer. Achieves 35% higher accuracy than RAG baseline on ScreenshotVQA while reducing storage by 99.9%.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — directly addresses AgentForge's no-memory-across-runs gap, but the full MIRIX architecture is heavyweight. Karpathy ratchet loop (HID-116) covers the most impactful short-term use case first.

source: LangMem SDK for Agent Long-Term Memory
summary: LangChain's SDK providing episodic, semantic, and procedural memory types built on LangGraph's persistent StateGraph store. Supports cross-run persistence with namespacing by user/team/app_id; pluggable backends including PostgreSQL/pgvector.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge already has PostgreSQL; LangMem's procedural memory (agents updating their own system prompts) could improve prompt quality over time. Overlaps with MIRIX; defer until HID-116 ratchet loop delivers.

source: ACP: Unified Agent Communication Protocol for A2A Orchestration
summary: Proposed standard for Agent-to-Agent communication supporting decentralized identity verification, semantic intent mapping, and automated service-level agreements. Enables propose/accept/counter-offer negotiation patterns across heterogeneous agents.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — AgentForge uses single-agent-per-stage sequential execution with no inter-agent communication. ACP is relevant only if AgentForge moves to a multi-agent-per-stage model, which is out of current scope.

source: Self-Improving Coding Agents via Telemetry and Execution Traces
summary: Pattern where coding agents query their own runtime telemetry via a DSL to verify changes, identify regressions, and iterate using trace data as evidence. Agents self-improve by acting on structured execution feedback rather than just output text.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — maps to AgentForge's missing agent performance tracking and ratchet loop concept. Partially addressed by HID-116 (ExecutionReport injection). Full telemetry DSL would be a separate larger effort.

source: Speculative Actions: Lossless Framework for Faster Agentic Systems
summary: Uses faster/smaller models to predict and pre-execute likely future agent actions in parallel while the main LLM generates its response; commits only when predictions match the ground-truth action. Lossless speedup with no accuracy degradation.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — requires API-level model access for the speculative drafting model; AgentForge agents are opaque CLI subprocesses, making it impossible to intercept and pre-execute future actions at the orchestrator level.

summary: Technique where agents use idle time between tool calls to speculatively pre-plan next steps using draft models, reducing end-to-end latency by utilizing otherwise wasted compute cycles.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — same feasibility barrier as PASTE; AgentForge cannot observe idle periods within black-box CLI subprocess execution.
